title: Enhanced back-to-back security procedure handling
abstract: Aspects of the disclosure provide for a method of efficiently implementing security configurations for multiple domains between a network entity and a UE in a wireless system. For instance, this disclosure provides for a network entity sending to a UE a first security mode command for a first domain, receiving a first security mode complete message in response to the first security mode command, and sending a second security mode command for a second domain. In an aspect, the second security mode command is sent in response to receiving the first security mode complete message. In an aspect, the UE does not receive a RLC acknowledgement. In another aspect, the UE saves the second security mode command until it receives the RLC acknowledgement. In an aspect, the network entity sends the second security mode command in response to receiving a message protected with first security procedure parameters.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method of implementing a security mode configuration in a wireless system, the method comprising: sending, by a network entity in the wireless system to a user equipment (UE), a first security mode command for a first domain; receiving, a first security mode complete message sent from the UE in response to the first security mode command; and sending, to the UE, a second security mode command for a second domain. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the second security mode command is sent before a first Radio Link Control (RLC) acknowledgement associated with the first security mode complete message is received by the UE, or wherein the method further comprises refraining from sending the first RLC acknowledgement to the UE. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein at least one of the first domain or the second domain comprises a circuit-switched (CS) domain. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein at least one of the first domain or the second domain comprises a packet-switched (PS) domain. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the second security mode command for the second domain is sent in response to receiving the first security mode complete message. 6. The method of claim 1, further comprising: 

 sending a first RLC acknowledgement in response to the first security mode command; and receiving, from the UE, a message protected with first security procedure parameters associated with the first domain, wherein the second security mode command for the second domain is sent in response to receiving the message protected with the first security procedure parameters. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the message protected with first security procedure parameters associated with the first domain comprises: a measurement report, the measurement report generated based upon the receipt of the RLC acknowledgement. 8. A method of implementing a security mode configuration in a wireless system, the method comprising: receiving, by a user equipment (UE), a first security mode command for a first domain sent by a network entity in the wireless system; sending, to the network entity, a first security mode complete message in response to receiving the first security mode command; and receiving a second security mode command for a second domain. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the second security mode command is received before receiving a first Radio Link Control (RLC) acknowledgement associated with the first security mode complete message, or wherein the first RLC acknowledgement is not received. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising: 

 concluding a first security mode procedure for the first domain, the conclusion of the first security mode procedure based upon reception of the second security mode command. 11. The method of claim 8, further comprising: saving, by the UE, the second security mode command; receiving a first Radio Link Control (RLC) acknowledgement sent by the network entity in response to the first security mode complete message; and processing, by the UE, the second security mode command after receiving the first RLC acknowledgement. 12. The method of claim 8, wherein the second security mode command for the second domain is received in response to the sending of the first security mode complete message. 13. The method of claim 8, further comprising: receiving, at the UE, a first RLC acknowledgement associated with the first security mode complete message; and sending, from the UE, a message protected with first security procedure parameters associated with the first domain, wherein the second security mode command for the second domain is received from the network in response to the message protected with the first security procedure parameters. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the message protected with the first security procedure parameters associated with the first domain comprises: a measurement report, the measurement report generated based upon the receipt of the RLC acknowledgement. 15. An apparatus for implementing a security mode configuration in a wireless system, the apparatus comprising: at least one processor; a receiver configured to receive messages; a transmitter configured to send messages; a memory; and a bus coupled to the at least one processor, receiver, transmitter, and memory, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: send to a user equipment (UE) a first security mode command for a first domain, receive a first security mode complete message sent from the UE in response to the first security mode command, and send a second security mode command for a second domain. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: send the second security mode command before a first Radio Link Control (RLC) acknowledgement associated with the first security mode complete message is received by the UE; or refrain from sending the first RLC acknowledgement to the UE. 17. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein at least one of the first domain or the second domain comprises a circuit-switched (CS) domain. 18. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein at least one of the first domain or the second domain comprises a packet-switched (PS) domain. 19. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to send the second security mode command for the second domain in response to receiving the first security mode complete message. 20. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: send a first RLC acknowledgement in response to the first security mode complete message; receive a message protected with first security procedure parameters associated with the first domain from the UE; and send the second security mode command for the second domain in response to receiving the message protected with the first security procedure parameters. 21. The apparatus of claim 20, wherein the message protected with first security procedure parameters associated with the first domain comprises: a measurement report, the measurement report generated based upon the receipt of the RLC acknowledgement. 22. An apparatus for implementing a security mode configuration in a wireless system, the apparatus comprising: at least one processor; a received configured to receive messages; a transmitter configured to send messages; a memory; and a bus coupled to the at least one processor, receiver, transmitter, and memory, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: receive, via the receiver, a first security mode command for a first domain sent by a network entity in the wireless system; 

 send, via the transmitter, a first security mode complete message to the network entity in response to receiving the first security mode command, and receive, via the receiver, a second security mode command for a second domain. 23. The apparatus of claim 22, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to receive the second security mode command before receiving a first RLC acknowledgement associated with the first security mode complete message; or wherein the processor does not receive the first RLC acknowledgement. 24. The apparatus of claim 23, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: conclude a first security mode procedure for the first domain, the conclusion of the first security mode procedure based upon reception of the second security mode command. 25. The apparatus of claim 22, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: save the second security mode command in the memory; receive a first RLC acknowledgement sent by the network entity in response to the first security mode complete message; and process the second security mode command after receiving te first RLC acknowledgement. 26. The apparatus of claim 22, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: 

 receive the second security mode command for the second domain in response to the sending of the first security mode complete message. 27. The apparatus of claim 22, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: receive a first RLC acknowledgement associated with the first security mode complete message; and send a message protected with first security procedure parameters associated with the first domain, wherein the second security mode command for the second domain is received from the network entity in response to the message protected with the first security procedure parameters. 28. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the message protected with the first security procedure parameters associated with the first domain comprises: a measurement report, the measurement report generated based upon the receipt of the RLC acknowledgement.
